LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT DANIEL BERKO'S MOTION FOR AN ORDER COMPELLING THE SAN FRANCISCO SUPERIOR COURT TO PAY, FROM FUNDS HELD BY THE COURT, MONIES NECESSARY TO PAY THE JUDGMENT ENTERED IN THIS ACTION AGAINST LARRY DUBAN AND IN FAVOR OF JUDGMENT CREDITOR DANIEL BERKO AND THE TWO SANCTIONS AWARDS MADE IN THIS CASE. ARGUED FOR CLARIFICATION AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION GRANTED. PLAINTIFF DUBAN FAILED TO POST A BOND IN COMPLIANCE WITH CCP SECTION 917.1 BY DECEMBER 31, 2009. COURT SHALL RELEASE INTERPLEADED FUNDS FROM CASE NUMBER 08-476380 TO DEFENDANT BERKO IN AN AMOUNT EQUAL TO THE JUDGMENT, PLUS COSTS, PLUS THE TWO SANCTIONS ORDERS, PLUS ACCRUED INTEREST. RELEASE OF FUNDS APPROPRIATE BECAUSE THE INTERPLEADER ACTION INVOLVES DUBAN, BERKO, AND OMAN; THE FUNDS IN INTERPLEADER WERE THE RESULT OF A $300,000 SETTLEMENT IN THE WRONGFUL EVICTION CASE WHERE BERKO AND OMAN REPRESENTED DUBAN; BERKO PREVAILED IN THIS MALPRACTICE/FEES CASE AND OBTAINED A $150,000 JUDGMENT AND SANCTIONS ORDERS; DUBAN APPEALED THAT DECISION; AND OMAN DOES NOT OPPOSE THE RELEASE OF FUNDS TO BERKO. MR. BERKO IN OPEN COURT HAS SERVED MR. DURBAN WITH A COPY OF THE PROPOSED ORDER REGARDING TODAY'S HEARING. MR. DURBAN HAS FIVE DAYS TO SUBMIT HIS OBJECTIONS IN WRITING TO PROPOSED ORDER TO EITHER MR. BERKO OR DIRECTLY TO JUDGE WOOLARD. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; COURT REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE CSR 5797

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT DANIEL BERKO MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY AJUDICATION. PLAINTIFF IN PRO PER MR. DUBAN FAILED TO GIVE NOTICE, PURSUANT TO SAN FRANCISCO LOCAL RULE 8.3. THE COURT ALLOWS ARGUMENT. RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT GRANTED. DEFENDANT AND CROSS-COMPLAINANT SHIFTS THE BURDEN OF PROOF. PLAINTIFF AND CROSS-DEFENDANT FAILS TO RAISE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT. DEFENDANT'S AND CROSS-COMPLAINANT'S REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS DEEMED ADMITTED IN ORDERS DATED 9/18/08 AND 11/24/08. PLAINTIFF AND CROSS-DEFENDANT HAS ADMITTED THAT BERKO DID NOT CAUSE HIM ECONOMIC HARM, THE PARTIES HAVE A VALID CONTINGENCY FEE AGREEMENT, BERKO ACTED WITH REASONABLE CARE IN PROSECUTING PLAINTIFF'S CASE, PLAINTIFF HAS NO EVIDENCE HE WOULD HAVE OBTAINED MORE THAN $200,000.00 AND BERKO IS ENTITLED TO AT LEAST $119,928.00 IN FEES. SUMMARY JUDGMENT MAY BE GRANTED BASED UPON ADMISSIONS DEEMED ADMITTED. (SEE STEELE VS. TOTAH (1986) 180 CAL.APP.3D 545, 554.) DEFENDANT, CROSS-COMPLAINT IS ENTITLED TO PREJUDGMENT INTEREST. (CIVIL CODE SECTION 3287.) THE COURT SUSTAINS DEFENDANT'S AND CROSS-COMPLAINANT'S OBJECTIONS, BUT EVEN IF DUBAN'S DECLARATION IS CONSIDERED, NO TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT IS RAISED. THE COURT ADOPTS DEFENDANT'S AND CROSS-COMPLAINANT'S PROPOSED ORDER GRANTING MOTION. (ORDER AND JUDGMENT SIGNED IN OPEN COURT.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

LAW AND MOTION, 302, CROSS-DEFENDANT LARRY DUBAN'S DEMURRER TO CROSS-COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: OFF CALENDAR, FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED ON APRIL 9, 2008. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT DANIEL BERKO'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS AND MODIFIES ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED. THE GRAVAMEN OF THE COMPLAINT IS NOT CENTERED ON PROTECTED ACTIVITY. IT IS CENTERED ON DEFENDANT'S ALLEGED FAILURE TO EFFECTIVELY REPRESENT PLAINTIFF IN THE UNDERLYING CASE AND BREACH OF THE DUTY OF LOYALTY. THE ALLEGATIONS RELATED TO PROTECTED ACTIVITY ARE INCIDENTAL TO PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM. SEE FREEMAN V SCHACK (2007) 154 CAL APP 4TH 719, 727-733. PLAINTIFF'S ORAL REQUEST FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES IS DENIED. Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Compelling The San Francisco Superior Court To Pay, From Funds Held By The Court, Monies Necessary To Pay The Judgment Entered In Fthis Action Agaisnt Larry Duban And In Favor Of Judgment Creditor Daniel Berko And The Two Sanctions Awards Made In This Case; Declaration; Requesst For Judicial Notice
Off Calendar Jan-06-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT DANIEL BERKO'S Motion For An Order Compelling The San Francisco Superior Court To Pay, From Funds Held By The Court, Monies Necessary To Pay The Judgment Entered In this Action Against Larry Duban and in Favor of Judgment Creditor Daniel Berko And The Two Sanctions Awards Made In This Case. Appearances: Daniel Berko Defendant in Pro Per appearing and moving party; Larry Duban Defendant in Pro Per appearing and opposing party.Argued for clarification and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: MOTION GRANTED. PLAINTIFF DUBAN FAILED TO POST A BOND IN COMPLIANCE WITH CCP SECTION 917.1 BY DECEMBER 31, 2009. COURT SHALL RELEASE INTERPLEADED FUNDS FROM CASE NUMBER 08-476380 TO DEFENDANT BERKO IN AN AMOUNT EQUAL TO THE JUDGMENT, PLUS COSTS, PLUS THE TWO SANCTIONS ORDERS, PLUS ACCRUED INTEREST. RELEASE OF FUNDS APPROPRIATE BECAUSE THE INTERPLEADER ACTION INVOLVES DUBAN, BERKO, AND OMAN; THE FUNDS IN INTERPLEADER WERE THE RESULT OF A $300,000 SETTLEMENT IN THE WRONGFUL EVICTION CASE WHERE BERKO AND OMAN REPRESENTED DUBAN; BERKO PREVAILED IN THIS MALPRACTICE/FEES CASE AND OBTAINED A $150,000 JUDGMENT AND SANCTIONS ORDERS; DUBAN APPEALED THAT DECISION; AND OMAN DOES NOT OPPOSE THE RELEASE OF FUNDS TO BERKO. Mr. Berko in Open Court has served Mr. Durban with a copy of the proposed Order regarding today's hearing. Mr. Durban has five days to submit his objections in writing to proposed Order to either Mr. Berko or directly to Judge Woolard. (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Aug-14-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT DANIEL BERKO MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY AJUDICATION. Appearances: Daniel Berko, Esq. Law Offices of Daniel Berko, (415) 771-6174, appearing in Pro Per and moving party; Larry Duban, Plaintiff in Pro Per (541-331-5766) appearing and opposing party. Plaintiff in Pro Per Mr. Duban failed to give notice, pursuant to San Francisco Local Rule 8.3. The Court allows argument. Ruling: The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion for summary judgment granted. Defendant and cross-complainant shifts the burden of proof. Plaintiff and cross-defendant fails to raise a triable issue of material fact. Defendant's and cross-complainant's requests for admissions deemed admitted in orders dated 9/18/08 and 11/24/08. Plaintiff and cross-defendant has admitted that Berko did not cause him economic harm, the parties have a valid contingency fee agreement, Berko acted with reasonable care in prosecuting plaintiff's case, plaintiff has no evidence he would have obtained more than $200,000.00 and Berko is entitled to at least $119,928.00 in fees. Summary Judgment may be granted based upon admissions deemed admitted. (See Steele vs. Totah (1986) 180 Cal.App.3d 545, 554.) Defendant, cross-complaint is entitled to prejudgment interest. (Civil Code section 3287.) The court sustains defendant's and cross-complainant's objections, but even if Duban's declaration is considered, no triable issue of fact is raised. The Court adopts defendant's and cross-complainant's proposed Order granting motion. (Order and Judgment signed in open Court.)(302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797
